rationale: logical replication is the only untried method that satisfies the no-downtime constraint

tasks.description: Create a pglogical publication for the billing schema on the source database and confirm it is active.
tasks.success_criterion: SELECT from pg_publication shows the billing publication with all billing tables attached
tasks.intent_link: logical replication (the chosen no-downtime method) requires a source publication before anything can subscribe to it

tasks.method: subscription on target with initial sync
tasks.description: Create the subscription on the target Postgres instance and let the initial table sync finish.
tasks.success_criterion: pg_subscription_rel reports state 'r' (ready) for every billing table
tasks.intent_link: the migration intent requires data present on the target; this is the no-downtime path now that t1's publication exists

tasks.description: Compare row counts and per-table checksums between source and target for the billing schema.
tasks.success_criterion: Every billing table matches source in row count and checksum, proving the migration intent holds
tasks.intent_link: the intent is a verified migration, not just a completed sync — this is the mandatory final verification task
